pattern: Two independent walks closing in from opposite ends -- simulate each robot's tank with its own capacity, refilling on demand, and only special-case the single middle plant they can both reach.
hints: Alice and Bob move toward each other at the same speed, so track two pointers and two remaining-water counters at once.
Whenever a robot's tank has less water than the next plant needs, it refills to full capacity first -- that refill always counts, even if the plant only needed a little more.
If the two robots end up at the very same plant (odd-length array), whichever of them is carrying more water tries it -- only refill once more if that larger amount still isn't enough.

edge_inputs.description: single plant, both capacities exactly cover it
single plant, neither capacity is enough, forces one refill
odd count, robots meet on the middle plant, tie in leftover water
tight capacities force a refill on almost every step for one robot
asymmetric capacities, one robot refills far more than the other
capacity exactly equal to the largest plant demand throughout
odd count, middle plant needs more water than either robot has left

stress.description: random plant demands with capacities guaranteed to cover the largest demand
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    plants = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    cap = max(plants)
    capacity_a = rng.randint(cap, cap + 1000000)
    capacity_b = rng.randint(cap, cap + 1000000)
    return [plants, capacity_a, capacity_b]
